evals.prompt: I need to prepare a 2-day Python basics workshop for complete beginners — people who have never coded before. They're marketing department staff at a mid-size company who want to automate some of their reporting tasks. We'll be using Python 3.12. Please start building the course — just do Milestone 1 (the blueprint) for now.
evals.expected_output: A well-structured course blueprint in _blueprint.md with: clear learning objectives appropriate for non-programmers, module breakdown covering Python basics through simple automation, realistic 2-day pacing, verified Python 3.12 references, and marketing-relevant examples. Should pause and ask for approval after presenting the blueprint.

evals.prompt: I'm creating a 3-module advanced React course for experienced JavaScript developers. Modules should cover: (1) React Server Components & Next.js App Router, (2) State management patterns (Zustand, Jotai, signals), (3) Performance optimization. Target: 6 hours total. Go through Milestones 1-3 for Module 1 only — I want to see the full output for one module before continuing.
evals.expected_output: Blueprint covering all 3 modules with time allocation, then detailed outline for module 1, then full source content for module 1 including: lecture.md with Obsidian-compatible markdown, code samples that reference actual React/Next.js APIs (verified), tasks with real-world exercises, and quiz questions. Should have validation markers and verified URLs. Pauses for approval at each milestone.

evals.prompt: Hey, I have a quick one — I need a quiz for my existing SQL course. The quiz should cover JOINs (INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL), subqueries, and GROUP BY with HAVING. About 15 questions, mix of multiple choice and write-the-query. I need both the markdown source and a Moodle GIFT export.
evals.expected_output: A quiz.md file with 15 well-crafted SQL questions covering the specified topics, with answers in a separate section. Then a GIFT format export file that Moodle can import. Questions should use realistic table schemas and test understanding, not just syntax recall. SQL syntax should be standard and correct.
